title: nanousd Backend Benchmark Reporting Capability
purpose: Define the reporting shape for comparing generated skillgraph nanousd backend benchmark results against nanousd default or other baseline backends.

included: Markdown best-run metric tables by target and backend
declared comparison tables with PASS, SLOW, SKIP, or FAIL status
optional inline SVG bar charts for one selected metric
deferred: statistical significance testing beyond the benchmark runner's best-run selection

required.reason: Reports interpret benchmark results for the generated nanousd backend and must preserve the backend contract's correctness/performance boundary.

input.baseline: backend id used as denominator for pressure ratios
input.candidate: backend id used as numerator for pressure ratios

output.chart: grouped bar chart by benchmark target and backend

laws: reporting consumes benchmark JSON and must not rerun benchmarks or parse benchmark stdout
best-run values in reports must come from the benchmark runner's best field, not from recomputing a different selection metric
reports must distinguish optional SLOW comparisons from required failures
tables and charts must include backend ids, target ids, metric names, and units where units are known
generated-vs-baseline pressure ratios must preserve the configured baseline and candidate backend ids
SVG reports must be self-contained and must not require external assets or JavaScript
report outputs are local review artifacts and must not be tracked unless a later contract explicitly promotes a stable fixture report
